steps.title: Identify the counter-explanation
steps.content: Stewart's notes provide an explicit alternative to Thucydides's explanation: a “clash of cultures” caused the conflict, not the rise of Athens. To challenge Thucydides, the quotation must capture this counter-explanation.

steps.title: Match it to the choices
steps.content: Choice C directly quotes Stewart's counter-explanation—the conflict was caused by a “clash of cultures,” not “Athens's rise”—exactly contradicting Thucydides's claim. The other three either restate Allison's theory (which supports rather than challenges Thucydides), vaguely mention a challenge without specifying it, or incorrectly claim Allison disagreed with Thucydides. That matches choice C — C is correct.
